messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - This seminar offers valuable insights into robust methods for improving your data analysis and survey estimations. Bregman Projection for Calibration Estimation (Free Seminar) is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 2 with Wei Fu (Merck), Jae-Kwang Kim (Department of Statistics, Iowa State University), and Pushpal Mukhopadhyay (Energy Information Administration and Washington Statistical Society). This seminar introduces calibration weighting as a Bregman projection, offering a unified framework to integrate auxiliary information into survey estimation and mitigate selection bias, which is crucial for robust epidemiological studies. Researchers in epidemiology can apply these techniques to improve the accuracy and efficiency of their analyses, especially when dealing with complex survey data or high-dimensional covariates in R. The framework supports debiased prediction and doubly robust estimation, enabling more reliable inferences from observational and survey-based epidemiological data.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/bregman-projection-for-calibration-estim) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Machine Learning for Computational Biology
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Machine Learning for Computational Biology
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - We invite you to explore an upcoming seminar on advanced analytical techniques relevant to your research. Machine Learning for Computational Biology 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 14 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar provides a comprehensive understanding of machine learning algorithms, including neural networks, random forests, k-means clustering, and MCMC methods. Participants will gain hands-on experience coding these algorithms from scratch using R and Python, focusing on practical applications for complex biological data. These skills can be directly applied by R-sig-Epi researchers to enhance their statistical analysis and display of epidemiological data, offering powerful new tools for their own projects.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/machine-learning-for-computational-biolo-2) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Causal Inference with LLMs: Apply to Your Data
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Causal Inference with LLMs: Apply to Your Data
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Explore how recent advancements can transform your approach to complex data analysis and causal inquiry. Causal Inference with LLMs is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 8 and 10 with Moses Boudourides (Data Science Graduate Program, School of Professional Studies, Northwestern University). Epidemiologists often work with complex data, including textual and mixed-format datasets, where traditional causal inference methods can face challenges in measurement and discovery. This seminar introduces how large language models (LLMs) can be rigorously integrated into causal inference workflows, offering new possibilities for extracting, constructing, and analyzing variables from these diverse data sources. Through reproducible scripts in Python and R, researchers can gain practical skills to formalize causal questions in LLM-augmented designs, audit bias, and validate outputs, thereby strengthening their ability to produce publication-ready causal analyses in their own work.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/causal-inference-with-llms) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R | 13–17 April
messages.subject_clean: (Online course) Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R | 13–17 April
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
it is still possible to join us for the upcoming online course, Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R, taking place from 13?17 April.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ )
 
 
This course provides a comprehensive and practical introduction to the core statistical concepts and methods used in clinical and biomedical research. Through a hands-on approach in R, participants will learn how to explore, analyze, and interpret medical data, while gaining a solid understanding of key techniques such as hypothesis testing, ANOVA, regression models, and survival analysis.
 
Particular emphasis is placed on selecting appropriate statistical methods, correctly interpreting results, and ensuring reproducible and transparent data analysis in line with modern publication standards.
 
The course is suitable for researchers, clinicians, and students who wish to strengthen their statistical skills and improve the quality of their data analysis and reporting.
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Project Quality & Management Best Practices
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Project Quality & Management Best Practices
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Discover best practices for managing data analysis projects effectively and ensuring high-quality outcomes. Project Quality and Management for Statisticians and Data Scientists is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 8 and 22 with Michiko Wolcott (Msight Analytics). This seminar offers best practices and methodologies to address non-statistical challenges in data analysis projects, such as data quality issues and planning under uncertainty. Epidemiological researchers can apply these project management principles to enhance the rigor, reproducibility, and transparency of their R-based analyses and studies. Learn practical tools and tactics to improve project robustness and delivery outcomes, directly benefiting the execution and reporting of your epidemiological research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/project-quality-and-management-for-stati) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: RNA-seq: From Counts to Conclusions
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: RNA-seq: From Counts to Conclusions
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Explore practical, statistically rigorous RNA-seq workflows to advance your genomic investigations. RNA-seq: From Counts to Conclusions is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 20 and 22 with Estefania Mancini (Josep Carreras Leukaemia Research Institute). RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) provides critical insights into gene expression, a powerful tool for epidemiological researchers seeking to understand molecular underpinnings of health and disease. This workshop offers practical, statistically rigorous RNA-seq workflows in R/Bioconductor, enabling you to translate raw sequencing data into robust, reproducible findings for your studies. You will gain skills to construct complete analysis pipelines, choose appropriate statistical models, and prepare publication-quality results, directly enhancing your ability to integrate genomic insights into epidemiological investigations.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/rna-seq-from-counts-to-conclusions) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Regression Modeling Strategies for Epidemiological Data
messages.subject_clean: Regression Modeling Strategies for Epidemiological Data
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

This seminar will equip you with advanced strategies to strengthen your empirical modeling and analysis practices. Regression Modeling Strategies is a 2-day seminar livestreaming May 14 and 19, led by Frank Harrell (Department of Biostatistics, Vanderbilt University) and Drew Levy. This seminar provides contemporary strategies for specifying, fitting, validating, and interpreting multivariable regression models, directly applicable for R-sig-Epi researchers to enhance the robustness and credibility of their epidemiological data analyses. Participants will learn to address challenges like nonlinearity, overfitting, and missing data, and to effectively model complex outcomes such as survival and longitudinal data using R, thereby improving the rigor of their empirical work. These practical skills, including pre-specifying model complexity and visualizing fitted models, will empower researchers to produce more transparent, reproducible, and publishable results in their own studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/regression-modeling-strategies) to register, and we would appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - This workshop offers practical skills for integrating cutting-edge AI tools into your qualitative research workflows using R. AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R 2.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming April 17 with Kenneth Benoit (Singapore Management University) and Seraphine Maerz (School of Social and Political Science, University of Melbourne). This seminar introduces AI-powered qualitative analysis in R, offering methods to efficiently process diverse data types like patient narratives, survey responses, or policy documents. Researchers can apply these techniques to enhance their epidemiological studies by systematically coding and interpreting unstructured data, such as public health communication or social media trends related to health outcomes. You will learn to build reproducible analysis pipelines in R, ensuring transparency and scalability for integrating qualitative insights into your quantitative epidemiological frameworks.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-powered-qualitative-analysis-in-r-20) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] (Online course) Handling Missing Data in R - 22–24 April
messages.subject_clean: (Online course) Handling Missing Data in R - 22–24 April
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

We have just a few seats left for our upcoming online course ?Handling Missing Data in R?, taking place on 22?24 April (3?6 PM Berlin time).
 
Missing data are a common challenge in real-world analyses, and handling them correctly is essential to avoid bias and ensure robust results. In this course, you will learn how to diagnose missingness (MCAR, MAR, MNAR), explore patterns in your data, and apply both traditional and modern imputation methods in R?including multiple imputation (MICE), regression approaches, and advanced algorithms such as EM and Soft Impute.
 
By the end of the course, you?ll be able to confidently choose and implement appropriate strategies for handling missing data in your own research.
 
 
More details and registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/missing-data-with-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/missing-data-with-r/ ) 
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D
